What was your company's Experience Modification Rate (EMR)? Value must be in **decimal format**, for example .85.  Experience Modification Rate (EMR) Sheets can usually be obtained from your commercial insurance broker and/or your Workers Compensation insurance carrier. This may be issued at the state or national level.

Please upload your Experience Modification Rate (EMR) document for the given year. EMR documents must be issued by your Worker's Compensation carrier, be in a non-editable format and have an Effective Rating Date within the given year to be approved. Example: An EMR document uploaded for the year 2023 must have an EFFECTIVE DATE IN THE SAME YEAR 2023.

Did your company have recordable work related injuries or illnesses for the given year? OSHA defines Recordable Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses as those injuries and illnesses that result in death, loss of consciousness, days away from work, restricted work activity or job transfer, or medical treatment beyond first aid. You must also record any significant work-related injury or illness that is diagnosed by a physician or other licensed health care professional. You must record any work-related case involving cancer, chronic irreversible disease, a fractured or cracked bone, or a punctured eardrum. See 29 CFR 1904.7.

Most common selection is Corporate. This field needs to remain consistent from year to year. If you need to change your reporting level, please contact Avetta Support. Most Avetta clients prefer to view corporate statistics, and when necessary have discussions about regional or site statistics that differ from the corporate numbers. It is possible to have several regional accounts in Avetta. Regional/Divisional breakdowns require communication across the entire organization as other divisions may desire to be represented with one Avetta account.
Corporation: Usually composed of several work sites, divisions, or regions. Division: A major unit or section of an organization, typically one handling a particular kind of work. Can also include several sites. Region: A geographical area of the company, can also include several sites, where work is performed. Site: A specific location where work is performed.

If this is a corporate account, all locations and divisions must be reported under corporate logs.\nPlease indicate the location type for which your data is being reported. If you are reporting for your entire corporation, please select Corporate; otherwise, specify Division, Region, or Site. Reference the help text for definitions of each location type.

For the citations with violations entered above, were there any violations your company received classified as a Other Than Serious Violation? OSHA Other Than Serious Violation: A violation that has a direct relationship to job safety and health, but is not serious in nature, is classified as "other-than-serious".

For the citations with violations entered above, were there any violations your company received classified as a Serious Violation? OSHA Serious Violation: A serious violation exists when the workplace hazard could cause an accident or illness that would most likely result in death or serious physical harm, unless the employer did not know or could not have known of the violation.
No
Yes

For the citations with violations entered above, were there any violations your company received classified as a Willful Violation? OSHA Willful Violation: A willful violation is defined as a violation in which the employer either knowingly failed to comply with a legal requirement (purposeful disregard) or acted with plain indifference to employee safety.
No
Yes

For the citations with violations entered above, were there any violations your company received classified as a Failure to Abate Violation? OSHA Failure to Abate Violation: Any new violations discovered during a follow-up inspection will be cited, as well as any hazards which have not been abated by the abatement date so specified on the OSHA Notice. The latter violations will be cited in the form of a Failure to Abate Notice.
No
Yes

For the citations with violations entered above, were there any violations your company received classified as a De Minimis Violation? De Minimis Violation: De minimis conditions are those where the employer has implemented a measure different from one specified in a standard, that has no direct or immediate relationship to safety or health. These conditions do not result in citations or penalties.
